column1: Key Numbered Sets, Trials, and Sacred Trees
Conference of the Birds - 30 birds (si-murgh) reach Simurgh at the end of the journey3459.
(Each valley represents a spiritual trial or transformation459.)
Gaokerena (Haoma Tree of Life): Simurgh perches here; when she flies, seeds of all plants scatter, symbolizing healing and fertility1011.

Tree of Knowledge / Sacred Tree of Asha: In later legends and modern retellings, Simurgh is guardian and companion of the cosmic tree, representing wisdom and the interconnectedness of all beings81011.
The Simurgh is always linked with a sacred tree (Gaokerena/Haoma/Tree of Knowledge).
The most famous numbered sets are the 7 Valleys (spiritual trials) and the 30 birds that reach Simurgh in Attar’s Conference of the Birds.
In the Shahnameh, Simurgh aids heroes in three major trials and is associated with the cosmic mountain/tree motif.
These motifs persist in art and literature across centuries and cultures.

Chapter & Verse/Source Citation: Where possible, direct references to canonical texts or chapters are provided.

Comparative Motifs: Many myths and parables share themes across Eurasia, such as the chaoskampf (hero vs. serpent/dragon), death and resurrection, and the use of parables for moral or spiritual teaching123.
